title: Auto-heal shared symlinks on `sweech use` / `sweech run` invocation
description:
Even with `sweech doctor` (T-076), users will hit broken shared dirs again whenever sweech upgrades and the SHAREABLE_DIRS list expands. The friction surfaces as `Unknown command: /<skill>` errors deep inside a Claude session — by which point the user has lost context.

Fix: when sweech routes a profile invocation (`sweech use <name>`, `sweech run`, `<profile>` wrapper script), do a cheap symlink integrity check first. If symlinks for any SHAREABLE_DIRS entry are missing or dangling, repair them silently and log to ~/.sweech/audit.log (no interactive prompt unless --verbose).

Key constraint: must be FAST. Should add <50ms to invocation latency. Strategy:
- Cache a `profile-symlinks.fingerprint` keyed on (SHAREABLE_DIRS hash + sweech version) in the profile dir
- Skip the check entirely if fingerprint matches current
- If fingerprint missing or mismatched, run full reconcile (≈10ms for an existing-symlinks scenario)

This is the long-term version of T-076 — T-076 is the one-shot heal; T-077 is the always-on heal.

context.text: Without this, every sweech version bump that adds a new entry to SHAREABLE_DIRS leaves existing users silently broken until they hit the failure mode and have to remember `sweech doctor`.
Implemented wrapper fingerprint gating plus healProfileSharedDirs fingerprint/audit writes. Verified with focused Jest, full Jest, tsc, swift build, and isolated HOME E2E smoke.

acceptanceCriteria: Profile invocation runs a fingerprint check before launching the CLI
Fingerprint hit: zero filesystem operations, <5ms overhead
Fingerprint miss: full symlink reconcile, repair, write new fingerprint
Repair is logged to ~/.sweech/audit.log with timestamp + what was fixed
Test: bump SHAREABLE_DIRS, launch existing profile, confirm new symlinks appear silently
